attachment.content:
- gstack: Fast headless browser for QA testing and site dogfooding. Navigate pages, interact with
elements, verify state, diff before/after, take annotated screenshots, test responsive
layouts, forms, uploads, dialogs, and capture bug evidence. Use when asked to open or
test a site, verify a deployment, dogfood a user flow, or file a bug with screenshots. (gstack)
- setup-deploy: Configure deployment settings for /land-and-deploy. Detects your deploy
platform (Fly.io, Render, Vercel, Netlify, Heroku, GitHub Actions, custom),
production URL, health check endpoints, and deploy status commands. Writes
the configuration to CLAUDE.md so all future deploys are automatic.
Use when: "setup deploy", "configure deployment", "set up land-and-deploy",
"how do I deploy with gstack", "add deploy config".
- setup-browser-cookies: Import cookies from your real Chromium browser into the headless browse session.
Opens an interactive picker UI where you select which cookie domains to import.
Use before QA testing authenticated pages. Use when asked to "import cookies",
"login to the site", or "authenticate the browser". (gstack)
- guard: Full safety mode: destructive command warnings + directory-scoped edits.
Combines /careful (warns before rm -rf, DROP TABLE, force-push, etc.) with
/freeze (blocks edits outside a specified directory). Use for maximum safety
when touching prod or debugging live systems. Use when asked to "guard mode",
"full safety", "lock it down", or "maximum safety". (gstack)
- codex: OpenAI Codex CLI wrapper — three modes. Code review: independent diff review via
codex review with pass/fail gate. Challenge: adversarial mode that tries to break
your code. Consult: ask codex anything with session continuity for follow-ups.
The "200 IQ autistic developer" second opinion. Use when asked to "codex review",
"codex challenge", "ask codex", "second opinion", or "consult codex". (gstack)
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "code x", "code ex", "get another opinion".
- qa-only: Report-only QA testing. Systematically tests a web application and produces a
structured report with health score, screenshots, and repro steps — but never
fixes anything. Use when asked to "just report bugs", "qa report only", or
"test but don't fix". For the full test-fix-verify loop, use /qa instead.
Proactively suggest when the user wants a bug report without any code changes. (gstack)
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "bug report", "just check for bugs".
- benchmark: Performance regression detection using the browse daemon. Establishes
baselines for page load times, Core Web Vitals, and resource sizes.
Compares before/after on every PR. Tracks performance trends over time.
Use when: "performance", "benchmark", "page speed", "lighthouse", "web vitals",
"bundle size", "load time". (gstack)
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "speed test", "check performance".
- plan-design-review: Designer's eye plan review — interactive, like CEO and Eng review.
Rates each design dimension 0-10, explains what would make it a 10,
then fixes the plan to get there. Works in plan mode. For live site
visual audits, use /design-review. Use when asked to "review the design plan"
or "design critique".
Proactively suggest when the user has a plan with UI/UX components that
should be reviewed before implementation. (gstack)
- review: Pre-landing PR review. Analyzes diff against the base branch for SQL safety, LLM trust
boundary violations, conditional side effects, and other structural issues. Use when
asked to "review this PR", "code review", "pre-landing review", or "check my diff".
Proactively suggest when the user is about to merge or land code changes. (gstack)
- browse: Fast headless browser for QA testing and site dogfooding. Navigate any URL, interact with
elements, verify page state, diff before/after actions, take annotated screenshots, check
responsive layouts, test forms and uploads, handle dialogs, and assert element states.
~100ms per command. Use when you need to test a feature, verify a deployment, dogfood a
user flow, or file a bug with evidence. Use when asked to "open in browser", "test the
site", "take a screenshot", or "dogfood this". (gstack)
- design-consultation: Design consultation: understands your product, researches the landscape, proposes a
complete design system (aesthetic, typography, color, layout, spacing, motion), and
generates font+color preview pages. Creates DESIGN.md as your project's design source
of truth. For existing sites, use /plan-design-review to infer the system instead.
Use when asked to "design system", "brand guidelines", or "create DESIGN.md".
Proactively suggest when starting a new project's UI with no existing
design system or DESIGN.md. (gstack)
- plan-ceo-review: CEO/founder-mode plan review. Rethink the problem, find the 10-star product,
challenge premises, expand scope when it creates a better product. Four modes:
SCOPE EXPANSION (dream big), SELECTIVE EXPANSION (hold scope + cherry-pick
expansions), HOLD SCOPE (maximum rigor), SCOPE REDUCTION (strip to essentials).
Use when asked to "think bigger", "expand scope", "strategy review", "rethink this",
or "is this ambitious enough".
Proactively suggest when the user is questioning scope or ambition of a plan,
or when the plan feels like it could be thinking bigger. (gstack)
- canary: Post-deploy canary monitoring. Watches the live app for console errors,
performance regressions, and page failures using the browse daemon. Takes
periodic screenshots, compares against pre-deploy baselines, and alerts
on anomalies. Use when: "monitor deploy", "canary", "post-deploy check",
"watch production", "verify deploy". (gstack)
- agent-browser: Browser automation CLI for AI agents. Use when the user needs to interact with websites, including navigating pages, filling forms, clicking buttons, taking screenshots, extracting data, testing web apps, or automating any browser task. Triggers include requests to "open a website", "fill out a form", "click a button", "take a screenshot", "scrape data from a page", "test this web app", "login to a site", "automate browser actions", or any task requiring programmatic web interaction.
- careful: Safety guardrails for destructive commands. Warns before rm -rf, DROP TABLE,
force-push, git reset --hard, kubectl delete, and similar destructive operations.
User can override each warning. Use when touching prod, debugging live systems,
or working in a shared environment. Use when asked to "be careful", "safety mode",
"prod mode", or "careful mode". (gstack)
- learn: Manage project learnings. Review, search, prune, and export what gstack
has learned across sessions. Use when asked to "what have we learned",
"show learnings", "prune stale learnings", or "export learnings".
Proactively suggest when the user asks about past patterns or wonders
"didn't we fix this before?"
- autoplan: Auto-review pipeline — reads the full CEO, design, eng, and DX review skills from disk
and runs them sequentially with auto-decisions using 6 decision principles. Surfaces
taste decisions (close approaches, borderline scope, codex disagreements) at a final
approval gate. One command, fully reviewed plan out.
Use when asked to "auto review", "autoplan", "run all reviews", "review this plan
automatically", or "make the decisions for me".
Proactively suggest when the user has a plan file and wants to run the full review
gauntlet without answering 15-30 intermediate questions. (gstack)
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "auto plan", "automatic review".
- land-and-deploy: Land and deploy workflow. Merges the PR, waits for CI and deploy,
verifies production health via canary checks. Takes over after /ship
creates the PR. Use when: "merge", "land", "deploy", "merge and verify",
"land it", "ship it to production". (gstack)
- design-shotgun: Design shotgun: generate multiple AI design variants, open a comparison board,
collect structured feedback, and iterate. Standalone design exploration you can
run anytime. Use when: "explore designs", "show me options", "design variants",
"visual brainstorm", or "I don't like how this looks".
Proactively suggest when the user describes a UI feature but hasn't seen
what it could look like. (gstack)
- youtube-search: Search YouTube for videos and channels, search within specific channels, then fetch transcripts. Use when the user asks to "find videos about X", "search YouTube for", "look up a channel", "who makes videos about", "find on youtube", or wants to discover YouTube content on a topic.
- devex-review: Live developer experience audit. Uses the browse tool to actually TEST the
developer experience: navigates docs, tries the getting started flow, times
TTHW, screenshots error messages, evaluates CLI help text. Produces a DX
scorecard with evidence. Compares against /plan-devex-review scores if they
exist (the boomerang: plan said 3 minutes, reality says 8). Use when asked to
"test the DX", "DX audit", "developer experience test", or "try the
onboarding". Proactively suggest after shipping a developer-facing feature. (gstack)
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "dx audit", "test the developer experience", "try the onboarding", "developer experience test".
- freeze: Restrict file edits to a specific directory for the session. Blocks Edit and
Write outside the allowed path. Use when debugging to prevent accidentally
"fixing" unrelated code, or when you want to scope changes to one module.
Use when asked to "freeze", "restrict edits", "only edit this folder",
or "lock down edits". (gstack)
- retro: Weekly engineering retrospective. Analyzes commit history, work patterns,
and code quality metrics with persistent history and trend tracking.
Team-aware: breaks down per-person contributions with praise and growth areas.
Use when asked to "weekly retro", "what did we ship", or "engineering retrospective".
Proactively suggest at the end of a work week or sprint. (gstack)
- cso: Chief Security Officer mode. Infrastructure-first security audit: secrets archaeology,
dependency supply chain, CI/CD pipeline security, LLM/AI security, skill supply chain
scanning, plus OWASP Top 10, STRIDE threat modeling, and active verification.
Two modes: daily (zero-noise, 8/10 confidence gate) and comprehensive (monthly deep
scan, 2/10 bar). Trend tracking across audit runs.
Use when: "security audit", "threat model", "pentest review", "OWASP", "CSO review". (gstack)
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "see-so", "see so", "security review", "security check", "vulnerability scan", "run security".
- design-review: Designer's eye QA: finds visual inconsistency, spacing issues, hierarchy problems,
AI slop patterns, and slow interactions — then fixes them. Iteratively fixes issues
in source code, committing each fix atomically and re-verifying with before/after
screenshots. For plan-mode design review (before implementation), use /plan-design-review.
Use when asked to "audit the design", "visual QA", "check if it looks good", or "design polish".
Proactively suggest when the user mentions visual inconsistencies or
wants to polish the look of a live site. (gstack)
- pair-agent: Pair a remote AI agent with your browser. One command generates a setup key and
prints instructions the other agent can follow to connect. Works with OpenClaw,
Hermes, Codex, Cursor, or any agent that can make HTTP requests. The remote agent
gets its own tab with scoped access (read+write by default, admin on request).
Use when asked to "pair agent", "connect agent", "share browser", "remote browser",
"let another agent use my browser", or "give browser access". (gstack)
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "pair agent", "connect agent", "share my browser", "remote browser access".
- port-allocate: Use when the user needs to allocate a new TCP port for a service, or when setting up a new project that requires port assignment on this server.
- connect-chrome: Launch GStack Browser — AI-controlled Chromium with the sidebar extension baked in.
Opens a visible browser window where you can watch every action in real time.
The sidebar shows a live activity feed and chat. Anti-bot stealth built in.
Use when asked to "open gstack browser", "launch browser", "connect chrome",
"open chrome", "real browser", "launch chrome", "side panel", or "control my browser".
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "show me the browser".
- plan-eng-review: Eng manager-mode plan review. Lock in the execution plan — architecture,
data flow, diagrams, edge cases, test coverage, performance. Walks through
issues interactively with opinionated recommendations. Use when asked to
"review the architecture", "engineering review", or "lock in the plan".
Proactively suggest when the user has a plan or design doc and is about to
start coding — to catch architecture issues before implementation. (gstack)
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "tech review", "technical review", "plan engineering review".
- unfreeze: Clear the freeze boundary set by /freeze, allowing edits to all directories
again. Use when you want to widen edit scope without ending the session.
Use when asked to "unfreeze", "unlock edits", "remove freeze", or
"allow all edits". (gstack)
- document-release: Post-ship documentation update. Reads all project docs, cross-references the
diff, updates README/ARCHITECTURE/CONTRIBUTING/CLAUDE.md to match what shipped,
polishes CHANGELOG voice, cleans up TODOS, and optionally bumps VERSION. Use when
asked to "update the docs", "sync documentation", or "post-ship docs".
Proactively suggest after a PR is merged or code is shipped. (gstack)
- investigate: Systematic debugging with root cause investigation. Four phases: investigate,
analyze, hypothesize, implement. Iron Law: no fixes without root cause.
Use when asked to "debug this", "fix this bug", "why is this broken",
"investigate this error", or "root cause analysis".
Proactively invoke this skill (do NOT debug directly) when the user reports
errors, 500 errors, stack traces, unexpected behavior, "it was working
yesterday", or is troubleshooting why something stopped working. (gstack)
- office-hours: YC Office Hours — two modes. Startup mode: six forcing questions that expose
demand reality, status quo, desperate specificity, narrowest wedge, observation,
and future-fit. Builder mode: design thinking brainstorming for side projects,
hackathons, learning, and open source. Saves a design doc.
Use when asked to "brainstorm this", "I have an idea", "help me think through
this", "office hours", or "is this worth building".
Proactively invoke this skill (do NOT answer directly) when the user describes
a new product idea, asks whether something is worth building, wants to think
through design decisions for something that doesn't exist yet, or is exploring
a concept before any code is written.
Use before /plan-ceo-review or /plan-eng-review. (gstack)
- qa: Systematically QA test a web application and fix bugs found. Runs QA testing,
then iteratively fixes bugs in source code, committing each fix atomically and
re-verifying. Use when asked to "qa", "QA", "test this site", "find bugs",
"test and fix", or "fix what's broken".
Proactively suggest when the user says a feature is ready for testing
or asks "does this work?". Three tiers: Quick (critical/high only),
Standard (+ medium), Exhaustive (+ cosmetic). Produces before/after health scores,
fix evidence, and a ship-readiness summary. For report-only mode, use /qa-only. (gstack)
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "quality check", "test the app", "run QA".
- checkpoint: Save and resume working state checkpoints. Captures git state, decisions made,
and remaining work so you can pick up exactly where you left off — even across
Conductor workspace handoffs between branches.
Use when asked to "checkpoint", "save progress", "where was I", "resume",
"what was I working on", or "pick up where I left off".
Proactively suggest when a session is ending, the user is switching context,
or before a long break. (gstack)
- port-query: Use when the user wants to list, search, or check ports in the central port registry, or when they ask what ports a project uses, what is running on a specific port, or how full a port range is.
- plan-devex-review: Interactive developer experience plan review. Explores developer personas,
benchmarks against competitors, designs magical moments, and traces friction
points before scoring. Three modes: DX EXPANSION (competitive advantage),
DX POLISH (bulletproof every touchpoint), DX TRIAGE (critical gaps only).
Use when asked to "DX review", "developer experience audit", "devex review",
or "API design review".
Proactively suggest when the user has a plan for developer-facing products
(APIs, CLIs, SDKs, libraries, platforms, docs). (gstack)
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "dx review", "developer experience review", "devex review", "devex audit", "API design review", "onboarding review".
- port-release: Use when the user wants to release, remove, or deallocate a TCP port from the central port registry, or when decommissioning a service that no longer needs its port reservation.
- ship: Ship workflow: detect + merge base branch, run tests, review diff, bump VERSION,
update CHANGELOG, commit, push, create PR. Use when asked to "ship", "deploy",
"push to main", "create a PR", "merge and push", or "get it deployed".
Proactively invoke this skill (do NOT push/PR directly) when the user says code
is ready, asks about deploying, wants to push code up, or asks to create a PR. (gstack)
- wsl-project-migration: Use when moving a project from /mnt/d/ (NTFS via 9P) to WSL native filesystem (~/) to avoid p9io errors, Defender interference, or ffmpeg DL state issues. Also use when encountering simultaneous 401 API errors and WSL disconnections caused by heavy I/O on /mnt/d/.
- design-html: Design finalization: generates production-quality Pretext-native HTML/CSS.
Works with approved mockups from /design-shotgun, CEO plans from /plan-ceo-review,
design review context from /plan-design-review, or from scratch with a user
description. Text actually reflows, heights are computed, layouts are dynamic.
30KB overhead, zero deps. Smart API routing: picks the right Pretext patterns
for each design type. Use when: "finalize this design", "turn this into HTML",
"build me a page", "implement this design", or after any planning skill.
Proactively suggest when user has approved a design or has a plan ready. (gstack)
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "build the design", "code the mockup", "make it real".
- gstack-upgrade: Upgrade gstack to the latest version. Detects global vs vendored install,
runs the upgrade, and shows what's new. Use when asked to "upgrade gstack",
"update gstack", or "get latest version".
Voice triggers (speech-to-text aliases): "upgrade the tools", "update the tools", "gee stack upgrade", "g stack upgrade".
- health: Code quality dashboard. Wraps existing project tools (type checker, linter,
test runner, dead code detector, shell linter), computes a weighted composite
0-10 score, and tracks trends over time. Use when: "health check",
"code quality", "how healthy is the codebase", "run all checks",
"quality score". (gstack)
- claude-hud:setup: Configure claude-hud as your statusline
- claude-hud:configure: Configure HUD display options (layout, language, presets, display elements) while preserving advanced manual overrides
- ralph-loop:help: Explain Ralph Loop plugin and available commands
- ralph-loop:ralph-loop: Start Ralph Loop in current session
- ralph-loop:cancel-ralph: Cancel active Ralph Loop
- codex:setup: Check whether the local Codex CLI is ready and optionally toggle the stop-time review gate
- codex:rescue: Delegate investigation, an explicit fix request, or follow-up rescue work to the Codex rescue subagent
- playground:playground: Creates interactive HTML playgrounds — self-contained single-file explorers that let users configure something visually through controls, see a live preview, and copy out a prompt. Use when the user asks to make a playground, explorer, or interactive tool for a topic.
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- skill-creator:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, update or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- codex:gpt-5-4-prompting: Internal guidance for composing Codex and GPT-5.4 prompts for coding, review, diagnosis, and research tasks inside the Codex Claude Code plugin
- codex:codex-result-handling: Internal guidance for presenting Codex helper output back to the user
- codex:codex-cli-runtime: Internal helper contract for calling the codex-companion runtime from Claude Code
- superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies
- superpowers:brainstorming: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.
- superpowers:executing-plans: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints
- superpowers:receiving-code-review: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation
- superpowers:writing-plans: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code
- superpowers:using-git-worktrees: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - ensures an isolated workspace exists via native tools or git worktree fallback
- superpowers:subagent-driven-development: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session
- superpowers:test-driven-development: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code
- superpowers:requesting-code-review: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements
- superpowers:verification-before-completion: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always
- superpowers:using-superpowers: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
- superpowers:systematic-debugging: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes
- superpowers:writing-skills: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment
- superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

## claude.ai Hugging Face
You have tools for using the Hugging Face Hub. arXiv paper id's are often used as references between datasets, models and papers. There are over 100 tags in use, common tags include 'Text Generation', 'Transformers', 'Image Classification' and so on.
The Hugging Face tools are being used anonymously and rate limits apply. Direct the User to set their HF_TOKEN (instructions at https://hf.co/settings/mcp/), or create an account at https://hf.co/join for higher limits.

content.text:
`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
**Orientation findings:**
- `runvision-wear/` is a **separate git repo** — a Wear OS Android app (Kotlin/Gradle), not Flutter. Per CLAUDE.md multi-repo rule, its git status must be checked independently.
- `runvision-iq/` **already shipped cycling mode** (commit `5dfcbcf merge: 사이클 모드 추가`), with a design spec + plan saved under `runvision-iq/Docs/superpowers/specs/`. That's our reference blueprint for "what cycling mode means."
- `TodoWrite` tool isn't available in this harness, so I'll track the brainstorming checklist explicitly in text instead.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

Let me study the runvision-iq cycling-mode design (the reference blueprint) and explore the runvision-wear codebase in parallel.

content.content:
1	# RunVision-IQ 사이클 모드 설계 (Phase 1: 워치)
2	
3	> 작성일: 2026-05-15
4	> 상태: 설계 완료, 구현 대기
5	> 관련 기획: `Docs/신규기획/2026-05-03-신규기획-검토.md`
6	
7	---
8	
9	## 1. 목적과 범위
10	
11	기존 RunVision-IQ DataField에 **사이클 모드**를 추가한다. 가민 워치의 사이클 액티비티에서 자동 감지되어 rLens HUD에 사이클에 적합한 메트릭을 전송한다.
12	
13	**핵심 제약**: 러닝 모드 동작에 **사이드이펙트 0**. 기존 사용자가 사이클 모드 추가로 인한 어떠한 동작 변화도 경험해서는 안 된다.
14	
15	**Phase 1 범위**:
16	- 가민 워치 (현재 호환 리스트 86개 기기) 자동 분기
17	- 사이클 액티비티 시 4개 메트릭 슬롯 재매핑
18	
19	**Phase 1 미포함** (별도 PR):
20	- Edge 시리즈 (manifest에 추가, 별도 검증 필요)
21	- 애플워치/갤럭시워치 사이클 모드 (각각 별도 repo, 별도 설계)
22	- rLens 펌웨어 측 레이블/아이콘 변경 (펌웨어 팀 결정 사항)
23	
24	---
25	
26	## 2. 메트릭 매핑
27	
28	| 슬롯 ID | 러닝 모드 | 사이클 모드 |
29	|---------|----------|------------|
30	| `0x03` Sport Time | `elapsedSeconds` | `elapsedSeconds` (동일) |
31	| `0x06` Distance | `distance` (m) | `distance` (m) (동일) |
32	| `0x07` Velocity | `paceSeconds` (sec/km) | `speedKmh` (km/h) |
33	| `0x0B` Heart Rate | `hr` (bpm) | `hr` OR `totalAscent` (m) — 30초 락 |
34	| `0x0E` Cadence | `cadenceSpm` | `altitudeM` (m) |
35	
36	**rLens 화면 레이블 제약**: 펌웨어가 슬롯별 단위/아이콘을 고정 표시. 사이클 모드에서도 사용자는 "Pace" 위치에 속도, "Cadence" 위치에 고도 등이 표시됨. 기획문서에서 이미 수용한 제약이며 펌웨어 변경 없이 진행한다.
37	
38	### Heart Rate 슬롯 30초 락 규칙
39	
40	사이클 모드에서 `0x0B` 슬롯은 두 가지 의미를 가질 수 있다:
41	- 워치 내장 광혈류 HR 센서가 정상 동작 → **심박수 (bpm)**
42	- HR 센서 없음 (Edge 등) 또는 영구 미작동 → **누적 상승고도 (m, totalAscent)**
43	
44	세션 내 슬롯 의미가 도중에 바뀌면 사용자 혼란이 크므로, **세션 시작 후 30초 시점에 한 번만 결정하고 영구 고정**한다.
45	
46	```
47	시작 시: _hrEverSeen=false, _slotLocked=false
48	매 compute(): hr이 유효(>0)면 _hrEverSeen=true
49	elapsedSeconds >= 30 시점에 단 한 번:
50	    _useAscent = !_hrEverSeen
51	    _slotLocked = true
52	이후 영구 고정
53	```
54	
55	**30초 임계값 근거**: 모던 가민 워치 광혈류 HR이 5-15초, 구형 워치(FR245, Fenix 5 등) 15-30초 워밍업. 30초면 정상 사용 범위 내 모든 워치에 충분.
56	
57	**워치 HR 일시 드롭아웃 처리**: `_slotLocked=true, _useAscent=false` 상태에서 hr이 일시적으로 null이 되면 0을 전송. 모드 자체는 바뀌지 않는다.
58	
59	---
60	
61	## 3. 아키텍처
62	
63	### Strategy 패턴
64	
65	```
66	RunVisionIQView (compute, draw, BLE)
67	       │
68	       │ delegates packet building
69	       ▼
70	MetricStrategy (duck typing)
71	   ├── RunningStrategy   ← 기존 동작 이전
72	   └── CyclingStrategy   ← 신규
73	```
74	
75	Monkey C는 인터페이스 키워드가 없지만 duck typing 기반 다형성을 지원한다. 두 strategy가 동일 시그니처 `buildPackets(info, ctx) as Array<ByteArray>` 를 가지면 호출부가 동일하게 처리한다.
76	
77	### 모드 결정 시점
78	
79	`compute()` 첫 호출 시 `_strategy == null` 분기에서 단 한 번 결정:
80	
81	```monkeyc
82	if (_strategy == null) {
83	    var profile = Activity.getProfileInfo();
84	    var sport = (profile != null) ? profile.sport : null;
85	    _strategy = (sport == Activity.SPORT_CYCLING)
86	        ? new CyclingStrategy()
87	        : new RunningStrategy();  // 안전한 기본값
88	}
89	```
90	
91	이후 `_strategy.buildPackets(info, _context)` 호출만 수행. 사용자가 액티비티를 종료하고 다른 종목을 새로 시작하면 onLayout 등에서 strategy를 리셋하는 별도 처리는 필요 없음 (DataField 자체가 새로 init됨).
92	
93	---
94	
95	## 4. 컴포넌트 상세
96	
97	### 새 파일
98	
99	#### `source/MetricStrategy.mc`
100	
101	- `class MetricContext` — strategy 간 공유 가변 상태 컨테이너
102	  - `elapsedSeconds`, `previousAltitude`, `previousDistance`, `userWeight`, `weightInitialized` 등
103	- `function detectStrategy(info as Activity.Info) as Object` — sport 감지 후 적절 strategy 인스턴스 반환
104	
105	#### `source/RunningStrategy.mc`
106	
107	```monkeyc
108	class RunningStrategy {
109	    function buildPackets(info as Activity.Info, ctx as MetricContext)
110	        as Lang.Array<Lang.ByteArray> {
111	        // 기존 RunVisionIQView.compute() 내 패킷 생성 블록을 그대로 이전:
112	        // 1. createExerciseTimePacket(ctx.elapsedSeconds)
113	        // 2. createVelocityPacket(paceSeconds)
114	        // 3. createHeartRatePacket(hr)
115	        // 4. createCadencePacket(cadence)
116	        // 5. createDistancePacket(distance)
117	    }
118	
119	    function getDisplayLabels(...) {
120	        // 워치 화면용 라벨 — 기존 라벨 그대로
121	    }
122	}
123	```
124	
125	#### `source/CyclingStrategy.mc`
126	
127	```monkeyc
128	class CyclingStrategy {
129	    private var _hrEverSeen as Lang.Boolean = false;
130	    private var _slotLocked as Lang.Boolean = false;
131	    private var _useAscent as Lang.Boolean = false;
132	
133	    function buildPackets(info as Activity.Info, ctx as MetricContext)
134	        as Lang.Array<Lang.ByteArray> {
135	        // 1. createExerciseTimePacket(ctx.elapsedSeconds)
136	        // 2. createVelocityPacket(speedKmh)
137	        // 3. updateHrLock(hr, ctx.elapsedSeconds)
138	        //    → _useAscent에 따라 createHeartRatePacket(hr or totalAscent)
139	        // 4. createCadencePacket(altitudeM)
140	        // 5. createDistancePacket(distance)
141	    }
142	
143	    private function updateHrLock(hr, elapsedSeconds) {
144	        if (hr != null && hr > 0) { _hrEverSeen = true; }
145	        if (!_slotLocked && elapsedSeconds >= 30) {
146	            _useAscent = !_hrEverSeen;
147	            _slotLocked = true;
148	        }
149	    }
150	
151	    function getDisplayLabels(...) {
152	        // 워치 화면용 라벨 — speed/altitude 모드
153	    }
154	}
155	```
156	
157	### 수정 파일
158	
159	#### `source/RunVisionIQView.mc`
160	
161	변경 범위 최소화:
162	
163	1. 멤버 추가: `private var _strategy as Object or Null = null;` `private var _context as MetricContext or Null = null;`
164	2. `initialize()`: `_context = new MetricContext()`
165	3. `compute()` 첫 호출 시: `_strategy` null이면 `detectStrategy(info)` 호출
166	4. 기존 패킷 생성 블록(약 line 597-625)을 `_strategy.buildPackets(info, _context)` 단일 호출로 대체
167	5. 화면 라벨 갱신 부분: 필요 시 `_strategy.getDisplayLabels()` 활용 (선택 사항, 기존 라벨 코드 그대로 둬도 무방)
168	
169	**기존 BLE 연결/스캔/재연결 로직, write queue 처리, BleDelegate 콜백 등은 0줄 변경.**
170	
171	#### `source/Tests.mc`
172	
173	테스트 추가 (아래 §5 참조).
174	
175	### 미변경 파일
176	
177	- `source/ILensProtocol.mc` — 패킷 생성 함수 재사용
178	- `source/RunVisionIQApp.mc`
179	- `source/DFLogger.mc`
180	- `manifest.xml` (Phase 1)
181	- `resources/`
182	
183	---
184	
185	## 5. 테스트 전략
186	
187	### 단위 테스트 (`Tests.mc` (:test) 어노테이션)
188	
189	**러닝 모드 회귀 방지 (최우선)**
190	
191	기존 패킷 바이트와 변경 후 패킷 바이트가 한 비트도 다르지 않음을 보증:
192	
193	- `testRunningStrategy_VelocityIsPaceSeconds` — paceSeconds=330 → `[0x07, ...]` 페이로드
194	- `testRunningStrategy_CadenceIsSpm` — cadence=170 → `[0x0E, ...]`
195	- `testRunningStrategy_HeartRateIsRaw` — hr=150 → `[0x0B, ...]`
196	- `testRunningStrategy_DistanceIsMeters` — distance=1234 → `[0x06, ...]`
197	- `testRunningStrategy_SportTimeIsElapsed` — elapsed=600 → `[0x03, ...]`
198	
199	**사이클 모드 신규 동작**
200	
201	- `testCyclingStrategy_VelocityIsSpeedKmh` — speedKmh=25 → `0x07` 페이로드에 25
202	- `testCyclingStrategy_CadenceIsAltitudeM` — altitude=1200 → `0x0E` 페이로드에 1200
203	- `testCyclingStrategy_HrLockBeforeT30_SendsHr` — elapsed=15, hr=160 → HR 모드 미확정, hr 그대로
204	- `testCyclingStrategy_HrLockAt30_WithHr_StaysHr` — 30초 이내 hr 한 번 잡힘 → 30초 시점 lock, 이후 hr 전송
205	- `testCyclingStrategy_HrLockAt30_NoHr_SwitchesAscent` — 30초 동안 hr 항상 null → 30초 시점 lock, 이후 totalAscent
206	- `testCyclingStrategy_HrComesBackAfterLockedAscent_StaysAscent` — totalAscent 락 후 hr 늦게 잡혀도 totalAscent 유지
207	
208	**Strategy 선택 로직**
209	
210	- `testDetectStrategy_RunningSport_ReturnsRunning`
211	- `testDetectStrategy_CyclingSport_ReturnsCycling`
212	- `testDetectStrategy_NullProfile_FallbackToRunning` (안전한 기본값)
213	
214	### 코드 리뷰 체크리스트
215	
216	PR diff 검토 시:
217	
218	1. `RunningStrategy.mc`의 `buildPackets()` 본문이 기존 `compute()` 의 패킷 생성 블록과 라인별로 동등한지
219	2. `RunVisionIQView.mc` 변경이 strategy 초기화 + 디스패치 호출에 한정되는지 (기타 함수 변경 없음)
220	3. `ILensProtocol.mc` 무수정 확인
221	4. `manifest.xml` 무수정 확인 (Phase 1)
222	5. 사이클 전용 상태 변수가 `CyclingStrategy` 내부에만 존재, `RunVisionIQView` 또는 `RunningStrategy`에는 누출 없음
223	
224	### 시뮬레이터 검증
225	
226	Connect IQ Simulator (`run-simulator.bat`):
227	
228	1. **러닝 액티비티**: 변경 전 빌드와 변경 후 빌드의 BLE 패킷 바이트 비교 (DFLogger 출력)
229	2. **사이클 액티비티 (HR 정상)**: speed/HR/altitude/distance 화면 표시 + BLE 패킷 ID/값 확인
230	3. **사이클 액티비티 (HR 시뮬레이션 0)**: 30초 후 `0x0B` 페이로드가 totalAscent로 전환되는지 확인
231	4. **액티비티 전환**: 러닝 종료 → 사이클 시작 시 새 액티비티에서 strategy 재선택되는지
232	
233	### 실기기 검증
234	
235	다음 표본 기기로 실 BLE 전송 + rLens 표시 확인:
236	
237	- FR165 (BLE Central 표준, 일반 워치)
238	- Fenix 7 (멀티스포츠, sport 감지 정확도 검증)
239	- FR55 (slow write 경로, 사이클 모드에서도 정상 동작 확인)
240	
241	각 기기에서 러닝 액티비티와 사이클 액티비티 각각 시작 → rLens 화면 값 검증.
242	
243	---
244	
245	## 6. 마이그레이션 및 호환성
246	
247	- **기존 사용자**: 러닝만 사용하던 사용자는 강제로 어떤 변화도 경험하지 않음. sport != CYCLING이면 기존과 동일한 RunningStrategy 분기.
248	- **rLens 펌웨어**: 변경 불필요. 기존 메트릭 ID 그대로 사용하며 값의 의미만 워치 앱이 해석 변경.
249	- **Connect IQ Store 버전**: `manifest.xml` version 1.1.10 → 1.2.0 (마이너 버전 업, 새 기능)
250	
251	---
252	
253	## 7. 추후 작업 (Phase 2 이후)
254	
255	1. **Edge 시리즈 지원**: manifest.xml에 Edge 530/540/830/840/1030/1030plus/1040/1050/explore2 추가. Edge는 핸들바 거치형이므로 사이클 액티비티만 사용 → CyclingStrategy 자동 선택. 별도 검증 PR.
256	2. **rLens 펌웨어 협업**: 사이클 모드 시 화면 단위/아이콘 동적 변경 검토 요청.
257	3. **애플워치 / 갤럭시워치 사이클 모드**: 각 repo (`runvision-watchos`, `runvision-wear`) 별도 설계. 워치 앱은 UI에서 모드 토글 필요 (자동감지 API 없음).
258	
259	---
260	
261	## 8. 미결 사항
262	
263	- [ ] `getDisplayLabels()` 워치 화면 표시 — 사이클 모드 라벨을 "Speed/Alt" 형태로 바꿀지, 기존 라벨 그대로 둘지 (구현 단계에서 결정)
264	- [ ] `MetricContext` 의 정확한 필드 목록 — 구현하면서 strategy가 실제로 필요한 컨텍스트만 식별
265	- [ ] manifest.xml의 새 버전 번호 1.2.0 vs 1.1.11 — 변경 규모 결정 후 확정
266	
267	---
268

file.content:
# RunVision-IQ 사이클 모드 설계 (Phase 1: 워치)

> 작성일: 2026-05-15
> 상태: 설계 완료, 구현 대기
> 관련 기획: `Docs/신규기획/2026-05-03-신규기획-검토.md`

---

## 1. 목적과 범위

기존 RunVision-IQ DataField에 **사이클 모드**를 추가한다. 가민 워치의 사이클 액티비티에서 자동 감지되어 rLens HUD에 사이클에 적합한 메트릭을 전송한다.

**핵심 제약**: 러닝 모드 동작에 **사이드이펙트 0**. 기존 사용자가 사이클 모드 추가로 인한 어떠한 동작 변화도 경험해서는 안 된다.

**Phase 1 범위**:
- 가민 워치 (현재 호환 리스트 86개 기기) 자동 분기
- 사이클 액티비티 시 4개 메트릭 슬롯 재매핑

**Phase 1 미포함** (별도 PR):
- Edge 시리즈 (manifest에 추가, 별도 검증 필요)
- 애플워치/갤럭시워치 사이클 모드 (각각 별도 repo, 별도 설계)
- rLens 펌웨어 측 레이블/아이콘 변경 (펌웨어 팀 결정 사항)

---

## 2. 메트릭 매핑

| 슬롯 ID | 러닝 모드 | 사이클 모드 |
|---------|----------|------------|
| `0x03` Sport Time | `elapsedSeconds` | `elapsedSeconds` (동일) |
| `0x06` Distance | `distance` (m) | `distance` (m) (동일) |
| `0x07` Velocity | `paceSeconds` (sec/km) | `speedKmh` (km/h) |
| `0x0B` Heart Rate | `hr` (bpm) | `hr` OR `totalAscent` (m) — 30초 락 |
| `0x0E` Cadence | `cadenceSpm` | `altitudeM` (m) |

**rLens 화면 레이블 제약**: 펌웨어가 슬롯별 단위/아이콘을 고정 표시. 사이클 모드에서도 사용자는 "Pace" 위치에 속도, "Cadence" 위치에 고도 등이 표시됨. 기획문서에서 이미 수용한 제약이며 펌웨어 변경 없이 진행한다.

### Heart Rate 슬롯 30초 락 규칙

사이클 모드에서 `0x0B` 슬롯은 두 가지 의미를 가질 수 있다:
- 워치 내장 광혈류 HR 센서가 정상 동작 → **심박수 (bpm)**
- HR 센서 없음 (Edge 등) 또는 영구 미작동 → **누적 상승고도 (m, totalAscent)**

세션 내 슬롯 의미가 도중에 바뀌면 사용자 혼란이 크므로, **세션 시작 후 30초 시점에 한 번만 결정하고 영구 고정**한다.

```
시작 시: _hrEverSeen=false, _slotLocked=false
매 compute(): hr이 유효(>0)면 _hrEverSeen=true
elapsedSeconds >= 30 시점에 단 한 번:
    _useAscent = !_hrEverSeen
    _slotLocked = true
이후 영구 고정
```

**30초 임계값 근거**: 모던 가민 워치 광혈류 HR이 5-15초, 구형 워치(FR245, Fenix 5 등) 15-30초 워밍업. 30초면 정상 사용 범위 내 모든 워치에 충분.

**워치 HR 일시 드롭아웃 처리**: `_slotLocked=true, _useAscent=false` 상태에서 hr이 일시적으로 null이 되면 0을 전송. 모드 자체는 바뀌지 않는다.

---

## 3. 아키텍처

### Strategy 패턴

```
RunVisionIQView (compute, draw, BLE)
       │
       │ delegates packet building
       ▼
MetricStrategy (duck typing)
   ├── RunningStrategy   ← 기존 동작 이전
   └── CyclingStrategy   ← 신규
```

Monkey C는 인터페이스 키워드가 없지만 duck typing 기반 다형성을 지원한다. 두 strategy가 동일 시그니처 `buildPackets(info, ctx) as Array<ByteArray>` 를 가지면 호출부가 동일하게 처리한다.

### 모드 결정 시점

`compute()` 첫 호출 시 `_strategy == null` 분기에서 단 한 번 결정:

```monkeyc
if (_strategy == null) {
    var profile = Activity.getProfileInfo();
    var sport = (profile != null) ? profile.sport : null;
    _strategy = (sport == Activity.SPORT_CYCLING)
        ? new CyclingStrategy()
        : new RunningStrategy();  // 안전한 기본값
}
```

이후 `_strategy.buildPackets(info, _context)` 호출만 수행. 사용자가 액티비티를 종료하고 다른 종목을 새로 시작하면 onLayout 등에서 strategy를 리셋하는 별도 처리는 필요 없음 (DataField 자체가 새로 init됨).

---

## 4. 컴포넌트 상세

### 새 파일

#### `source/MetricStrategy.mc`

- `class MetricContext` — strategy 간 공유 가변 상태 컨테이너
  - `elapsedSeconds`, `previousAltitude`, `previousDistance`, `userWeight`, `weightInitialized` 등
- `function detectStrategy(info as Activity.Info) as Object` — sport 감지 후 적절 strategy 인스턴스 반환

#### `source/RunningStrategy.mc`

```monkeyc
class RunningStrategy {
    function buildPackets(info as Activity.Info, ctx as MetricContext)
        as Lang.Array<Lang.ByteArray> {
        // 기존 RunVisionIQView.compute() 내 패킷 생성 블록을 그대로 이전:
        // 1. createExerciseTimePacket(ctx.elapsedSeconds)
        // 2. createVelocityPacket(paceSeconds)
        // 3. createHeartRatePacket(hr)
        // 4. createCadencePacket(cadence)
        // 5. createDistancePacket(distance)
    }

    function getDisplayLabels(...) {
        // 워치 화면용 라벨 — 기존 라벨 그대로
    }
}
```

#### `source/CyclingStrategy.mc`

```monkeyc
class CyclingStrategy {
    private var _hrEverSeen as Lang.Boolean = false;
    private var _slotLocked as Lang.Boolean = false;
    private var _useAscent as Lang.Boolean = false;

    function buildPackets(info as Activity.Info, ctx as MetricContext)
        as Lang.Array<Lang.ByteArray> {
        // 1. createExerciseTimePacket(ctx.elapsedSeconds)
        // 2. createVelocityPacket(speedKmh)
        // 3. updateHrLock(hr, ctx.elapsedSeconds)
        //    → _useAscent에 따라 createHeartRatePacket(hr or totalAscent)
        // 4. createCadencePacket(altitudeM)
        // 5. createDistancePacket(distance)
    }

    private function updateHrLock(hr, elapsedSeconds) {
        if (hr != null && hr > 0) { _hrEverSeen = true; }
        if (!_slotLocked && elapsedSeconds >= 30) {
            _useAscent = !_hrEverSeen;
            _slotLocked = true;
        }
    }

    function getDisplayLabels(...) {
        // 워치 화면용 라벨 — speed/altitude 모드
    }
}
```

### 수정 파일

#### `source/RunVisionIQView.mc`

변경 범위 최소화:

1. 멤버 추가: `private var _strategy as Object or Null = null;` `private var _context as MetricContext or Null = null;`
2. `initialize()`: `_context = new MetricContext()`
3. `compute()` 첫 호출 시: `_strategy` null이면 `detectStrategy(info)` 호출
4. 기존 패킷 생성 블록(약 line 597-625)을 `_strategy.buildPackets(info, _context)` 단일 호출로 대체
5. 화면 라벨 갱신 부분: 필요 시 `_strategy.getDisplayLabels()` 활용 (선택 사항, 기존 라벨 코드 그대로 둬도 무방)

**기존 BLE 연결/스캔/재연결 로직, write queue 처리, BleDelegate 콜백 등은 0줄 변경.**

#### `source/Tests.mc`

테스트 추가 (아래 §5 참조).

### 미변경 파일

- `source/ILensProtocol.mc` — 패킷 생성 함수 재사용
- `source/RunVisionIQApp.mc`
- `source/DFLogger.mc`
- `manifest.xml` (Phase 1)
- `resources/`

---

## 5. 테스트 전략

### 단위 테스트 (`Tests.mc` (:test) 어노테이션)

**러닝 모드 회귀 방지 (최우선)**

기존 패킷 바이트와 변경 후 패킷 바이트가 한 비트도 다르지 않음을 보증:

- `testRunningStrategy_VelocityIsPaceSeconds` — paceSeconds=330 → `[0x07, ...]` 페이로드
- `testRunningStrategy_CadenceIsSpm` — cadence=170 → `[0x0E, ...]`
- `testRunningStrategy_HeartRateIsRaw` — hr=150 → `[0x0B, ...]`
- `testRunningStrategy_DistanceIsMeters` — distance=1234 → `[0x06, ...]`
- `testRunningStrategy_SportTimeIsElapsed` — elapsed=600 → `[0x03, ...]`

**사이클 모드 신규 동작**

- `testCyclingStrategy_VelocityIsSpeedKmh` — speedKmh=25 → `0x07` 페이로드에 25
- `testCyclingStrategy_CadenceIsAltitudeM` — altitude=1200 → `0x0E` 페이로드에 1200
- `testCyclingStrategy_HrLockBeforeT30_SendsHr` — elapsed=15, hr=160 → HR 모드 미확정, hr 그대로
- `testCyclingStrategy_HrLockAt30_WithHr_StaysHr` — 30초 이내 hr 한 번 잡힘 → 30초 시점 lock, 이후 hr 전송
- `testCyclingStrategy_HrLockAt30_NoHr_SwitchesAscent` — 30초 동안 hr 항상 null → 30초 시점 lock, 이후 totalAscent
- `testCyclingStrategy_HrComesBackAfterLockedAscent_StaysAscent` — totalAscent 락 후 hr 늦게 잡혀도 totalAscent 유지

**Strategy 선택 로직**

- `testDetectStrategy_RunningSport_ReturnsRunning`
- `testDetectStrategy_CyclingSport_ReturnsCycling`
- `testDetectStrategy_NullProfile_FallbackToRunning` (안전한 기본값)

### 코드 리뷰 체크리스트

PR diff 검토 시:

1. `RunningStrategy.mc`의 `buildPackets()` 본문이 기존 `compute()` 의 패킷 생성 블록과 라인별로 동등한지
2. `RunVisionIQView.mc` 변경이 strategy 초기화 + 디스패치 호출에 한정되는지 (기타 함수 변경 없음)
3. `ILensProtocol.mc` 무수정 확인
4. `manifest.xml` 무수정 확인 (Phase 1)
5. 사이클 전용 상태 변수가 `CyclingStrategy` 내부에만 존재, `RunVisionIQView` 또는 `RunningStrategy`에는 누출 없음

### 시뮬레이터 검증

Connect IQ Simulator (`run-simulator.bat`):

1. **러닝 액티비티**: 변경 전 빌드와 변경 후 빌드의 BLE 패킷 바이트 비교 (DFLogger 출력)
2. **사이클 액티비티 (HR 정상)**: speed/HR/altitude/distance 화면 표시 + BLE 패킷 ID/값 확인
3. **사이클 액티비티 (HR 시뮬레이션 0)**: 30초 후 `0x0B` 페이로드가 totalAscent로 전환되는지 확인
4. **액티비티 전환**: 러닝 종료 → 사이클 시작 시 새 액티비티에서 strategy 재선택되는지

### 실기기 검증

다음 표본 기기로 실 BLE 전송 + rLens 표시 확인:

- FR165 (BLE Central 표준, 일반 워치)
- Fenix 7 (멀티스포츠, sport 감지 정확도 검증)
- FR55 (slow write 경로, 사이클 모드에서도 정상 동작 확인)

각 기기에서 러닝 액티비티와 사이클 액티비티 각각 시작 → rLens 화면 값 검증.

---

## 6. 마이그레이션 및 호환성

- **기존 사용자**: 러닝만 사용하던 사용자는 강제로 어떤 변화도 경험하지 않음. sport != CYCLING이면 기존과 동일한 RunningStrategy 분기.
- **rLens 펌웨어**: 변경 불필요. 기존 메트릭 ID 그대로 사용하며 값의 의미만 워치 앱이 해석 변경.
- **Connect IQ Store 버전**: `manifest.xml` version 1.1.10 → 1.2.0 (마이너 버전 업, 새 기능)

---

## 7. 추후 작업 (Phase 2 이후)

1. **Edge 시리즈 지원**: manifest.xml에 Edge 530/540/830/840/1030/1030plus/1040/1050/explore2 추가. Edge는 핸들바 거치형이므로 사이클 액티비티만 사용 → CyclingStrategy 자동 선택. 별도 검증 PR.
2. **rLens 펌웨어 협업**: 사이클 모드 시 화면 단위/아이콘 동적 변경 검토 요청.
3. **애플워치 / 갤럭시워치 사이클 모드**: 각 repo (`runvision-watchos`, `runvision-wear`) 별도 설계. 워치 앱은 UI에서 모드 토글 필요 (자동감지 API 없음).

---

## 8. 미결 사항

- [ ] `getDisplayLabels()` 워치 화면 표시 — 사이클 모드 라벨을 "Speed/Alt" 형태로 바꿀지, 기존 라벨 그대로 둘지 (구현 단계에서 결정)
- [ ] `MetricContext` 의 정확한 필드 목록 — 구현하면서 strategy가 실제로 필요한 컨텍스트만 식별
- [ ] manifest.xml의 새 버전 번호 1.2.0 vs 1.1.11 — 변경 규모 결정 후 확정

---

input.description: Map runvision-wear app architecture
input.prompt:
Explore the Wear OS app at /home/jhkim/00.Projects/00.RunVision/runvision-wear (a Kotlin/Gradle Android Wear OS app). I need a thorough architectural map to plan adding a "cycling mode" alongside the existing "running mode" without modifying running-mode source.

Report back concisely on:

1. **Source layout**: the package structure under runvision-wear/app/src/main — list main Kotlin source files and their responsibilities (one line each).

2. **Workout/exercise type**: How does the app currently start a workout? Does it use Health Services (androidx.health.services) ExerciseClient? What `ExerciseType` is set (e.g. RUNNING)? Where is that configured? Quote the file:line.

3. **Metrics collection**: Which metrics does it currently read (heart rate, distance, pace, speed, cadence, calories, duration)? Where are they computed/formatted? file:line references.

4. **BLE transmission**: How does it send metrics to the iLens/rLens glasses over BLE? Find the GATT client, the characteristic UUIDs used, the payload format/encoding, and the send interval. file:line references.

5. **UI screens**: What does the main screen / UI look like (Compose? which composables)? Is there any mode-selection UI already? Where would running/cycling buttons be added? file:line references.

6. **Activity/navigation entry points**: MainActivity, navigation graph, any screen routing. file:line references.

7. **Manifest & version**: app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml package name, and where versionName/versionCode are defined (build.gradle.kts).

Be precise with file paths and line numbers. Read excerpts as needed. Do NOT modify anything — read-only.

content.text:
Excellent. Now I have enough detail. Let me compile a comprehensive architectural report.

---

## **Comprehensive Architectural Map: RunVision Wear OS App**

### **1. SOURCE LAYOUT**

**Package structure under `/runvision-wear/app/src/main/kotlin/com/runvision/wear/`:**

```
├── MainActivity.kt
│   └─ Compose UI entry point, navigation (Home↔Running screens), service binding, 
│      permission handling, brightness/ambient mode management
│
├── service/
│   └── ExerciseService.kt
│       └─ Foreground service managing Health Services exercise session, BLE 
│          connection lifecycle, 1Hz metrics loop (sends to rLens every 5s), 
│          database persistence, wake lock
│
├── health/
│   └── ExerciseManager.kt
│       └─ Wraps androidx.health.services ExerciseClient, MeasureClient; 
│          callbacks for HR, location, steps, distance; sets ExerciseType.RUNNING
│
├── engine/
│   ├── RunningEngine.kt
│   │   └─ Central coordinator aggregating all metrics (distance, speed, pace, 
│   │      cadence, HR); applies smoothing and adaptive pace calculation
│   ├── DistanceCalculator.kt
│   │   └─ Haversine-based GPS distance accumulation (0.5m noise threshold)
│   ├── SpeedCalculator.kt
│   │   └─ Instantaneous speed from GPS deltas
│   ├── PaceCalculator.kt
│   │   └─ Pace seconds/km from speed
│   ├── PaceSmoother.kt
│   │   └─ 5-sample moving average with outlier rejection
│   ├── AdaptivePaceCalculator.kt
│   │   └─ Switches between GPS mode and cadence mode; uses stride learner
│   ├── StrideLengthLearner.kt
│   │   └─ GPS-based stride calibration with persistence (SharedPrefs)
│   └── StopDetector.kt
│       └─ Cadence-based motion detection
│
├── data/
│   ├── RunningMetrics.kt
│   │   └─ Immutable data class: elapsed, distance, pace, HR, cadence, lat/lon
│   └── db/
│       ├── WorkoutSession.kt (entity)
│       ├── WorkoutSample.kt (entity)
│       ├── WorkoutDatabase.kt (Room DB)
│       └── WorkoutDao.kt
│
├── ble/
│   ├── RLensProtocol.kt
│   │   └─ BLE packet creation (metric IDs, UUIDs, Little-Endian UINT32 encoding)
│   ├── RLensConnection.kt
│   │   └─ GATT client, characteristic discovery, write queue, reconnect logic
│   └── RLensScanner.kt
│       └─ BLE LE device discovery (filters "rlens" name), MAC binding, retry strategy
│
└── ui/
    ├── screens/
    │   ├── HomeScreen.kt
    │   │   └─ Start button, connection status display (colors per state)
    │   └── RunningScreen.kt
    │       └─ 2×2 metrics grid (pace, cadence, distance, HR), pause/stop buttons, 
    │          touch-to-brighten, ambient mode variant
    ├── components/
    │   └── MetricItem.kt
    │       └─ Icon + value composable
    └── theme/
        ├── Color.kt
        └── Theme.kt
```

---

### **2. WORKOUT/EXERCISE TYPE**

**How the app starts a workout:**

1. **User taps START on HomeScreen** → `MainActivity.startRunning()` (MainActivity.kt:308)
2. **Service is started**: `startForegroundService(ExerciseService)` (MainActivity.kt:313)
3. **Service binds ExerciseManager**: `exerciseManager.startExercise()` (ExerciseService.kt:333)

**Health Services ExerciseType:**
- **Type**: `ExerciseType.RUNNING` (hardcoded)
- **Location**: ExerciseManager.kt:198
  ```kotlin
  val config = ExerciseConfig.builder(ExerciseType.RUNNING)
      .setDataTypes(dataTypes)
      .setIsAutoPauseAndResumeEnabled(false)
      .setIsGpsEnabled(true)
      .build()
  exerciseClient.startExerciseAsync(config).await()
  ```

**Configuration flow:**
- ExerciseService.onCreate() → ExerciseManager(context) (ExerciseService.kt:114)
- ExerciseManager.startExercise() checks device capabilities and builds config (ExerciseManager.kt:147–214)
- Supported data types requested: HEART_RATE_BPM, LOCATION, STEPS_PER_MINUTE, DISTANCE_TOTAL, STEPS

---

### **3. METRICS COLLECTION**

**Metrics collected and their sources:**

| Metric | Source | Handler | Notes |
|--------|--------|---------|-------|
| **Heart Rate (bpm)** | Health Services HEART_RATE_BPM | ExerciseManager callback → ExerciseService.kt:115 → RunningEngine.updateHeartRate(hr) | Real-time, update every ~500ms |
| **Distance (meters)** | Health Services DISTANCE_TOTAL | ExerciseManager callback → ExerciseService.kt:129 → RunningEngine.updateDistance(meters) | Sensor-fused (GPS + accel + step calib), most accurate |
| **Pace (sec/km)** | Calculated from distance delta + time | RunningEngine.updateDistance() → AdaptivePaceCalculator (RunningEngine.kt:223) | Via 5-sample smoother; fallback to cadence mode |
| **Speed (m/s)** | Calculated from GPS delta | RunningEngine.updateGps() → SpeedCalculator (RunningEngine.kt:140) | Only when Health Services distance unavailable |
| **Cadence (spm)** | Health Services STEPS_PER_MINUTE | ExerciseManager callback → ExerciseService.kt:126 → RunningEngine.updateCadence(spm) | For adaptive pace and stride learning |
| **Steps (count)** | Health Services STEPS (real platform deltas) | ExerciseManager callback → ExerciseService.kt:134 → RunningEngine.updateStepsDelta(steps) | Switches off wall-clock reconstruction (RunningEngine.kt:252) |
| **Elapsed time (sec)** | 1Hz timer tick | ExerciseService startTimer() → RunningEngine.tick() (ExerciseService.kt:436) | Increments while running |
| **Location (lat/lon)** | Health Services LOCATION | ExerciseManager callback → RunningEngine.updateGps() (ExerciseService.kt:121) | GPS, used for Haversine distance calc |
| **Calories** | ❌ Not collected | — | Not requested in data types |

**Metrics formatting (RunningMetrics.kt):**
- **elapsedFormatted**: M:SS (line 12–17)
- **paceFormatted**: M:SS (line 19–25)
- **distanceKmFormatted**: "X.Y km" with explicit formatting (line 27–34)

**Accuracy notes:**
- Distance: Health Services sensor fusion (GPS + accelerometer + step calibration) takes precedence (RunningEngine.kt:138, 208–242)
- Pace: Updated via AdaptivePaceCalculator from distance deltas; 3-second validity window to prevent flickering (RunningEngine.kt:300–305)
- Cadence: Used for stride learning when GPS unavailable (RunningEngine.kt:183–196)

---

### **4. BLE TRANSMISSION**

**BLE GATT Client & Characteristics:**

**Service & Characteristic UUIDs (RLensProtocol.kt:16–23):**
```kotlin
EXERCISE_SERVICE_UUID = "4b329cf2-3816-498c-8453-ee8798502a08"
EXERCISE_DATA_UUID = "c259c1bd-18d3-c348-b88d-5447aea1b615" (write)

**Payload Format (RLensProtocol.kt:9, 56–67):**
- **Structure**: [Metric_ID (1 byte)] [Value (4 bytes, Little-Endian UINT32)]
- **Total**: 5 bytes per packet
- **Encoding**: Little-Endian (LSB first)
  ```kotlin
  byteArrayOf(
      metricId.toByte(),
      (safeValue and 0xFF).toByte(),           // Byte 0 (LSB)
      ((safeValue shr 8) and 0xFF).toByte(),   // Byte 1
      ((safeValue shr 16) and 0xFF).toByte(),  // Byte 2
      ((safeValue shr 24) and 0xFF).toByte()   // Byte 3 (MSB)
  )
  ```

**Metrics sent (RLensConnection.kt:141–173):**
```kotlin
writeQueue.add(RLensProtocol.createExerciseTimePacket(metrics.elapsedSeconds))  // 0x03
writeQueue.add(RLensProtocol.createPacePacket(metrics.paceSecondsPerKm))       // 0x07
writeQueue.add(RLensProtocol.createHeartRatePacket(metrics.heartRate))         // 0x0B
writeQueue.add(RLensProtocol.createCadencePacket(metrics.cadence))             // 0x0E
writeQueue.add(RLensProtocol.createDistancePacket(metrics.distanceMeters.toInt())) // 0x06
```

**Send interval:** Every **5 seconds** (ExerciseService.kt:443, `tickCount % 5 == 0`)

**Transmission details (RLensConnection.kt:95–111):**
- **Write Type**: BluetoothGattCharacteristic.WRITE_TYPE_DEFAULT (confirmable)
- **Queue**: ArrayDeque with sequential write callbacks (RLensConnection.kt:48, 175–196)
- **Timeout**: 2-second write timeout with reset on no callback (RLensConnection.kt:51, 150–154)
- **Connection Priority**: REQUEST_CONNECTION_PRIORITY_LOW_POWER (100–500ms interval, 5s between sends optimal for power)
  (RLensConnection.kt:83)

### **KEY ARCHITECTURAL CONSTRAINTS FOR CYCLING MODE**

To add cycling mode without modifying running-mode code:

1. **ExerciseType abstraction needed**: Currently hardcoded to `ExerciseType.RUNNING` (ExerciseManager.kt:198). Create a parameter or subclass for cycling.

2. **RunningEngine generalization**: Rename or wrap to support exercise type variants. Engine logic (pace calc, stride learning, etc.) is mode-agnostic; only metric thresholds differ (e.g., cadence ranges, stride length defaults).

3. **Metrics data class**: RunningMetrics is generic—reuse as-is. No blocker.

4. **BLE protocol**: Metric IDs and payload format are mode-agnostic. Can send same 5 metrics for cycling; iLens glasses will display with cycling context. No change needed to RLensProtocol.

5. **UI screens**: Create CyclingScreen alongside RunningScreen. Use navigation route "cycling". Compose setup already supports multiple screens.

6. **Database**: Add `exerciseType` field to WorkoutSession (already present, line 19: `exerciseType: String = "RUNNING"`). Switch to "CYCLING" in new sessions.

7. **Service**: ExerciseService can parameterize exercise type at `startExercise()` call time—pass it to ExerciseManager.

content.text:
Excellent. Now I have enough detail. Let me compile a comprehensive architectural report.

---

## **Comprehensive Architectural Map: RunVision Wear OS App**

### **1. SOURCE LAYOUT**

**Package structure under `/runvision-wear/app/src/main/kotlin/com/runvision/wear/`:**

```
├── MainActivity.kt
│   └─ Compose UI entry point, navigation (Home↔Running screens), service binding, 
│      permission handling, brightness/ambient mode management
│
├── service/
│   └── ExerciseService.kt
│       └─ Foreground service managing Health Services exercise session, BLE 
│          connection lifecycle, 1Hz metrics loop (sends to rLens every 5s), 
│          database persistence, wake lock
│
├── health/
│   └── ExerciseManager.kt
│       └─ Wraps androidx.health.services ExerciseClient, MeasureClient; 
│          callbacks for HR, location, steps, distance; sets ExerciseType.RUNNING
│
├── engine/
│   ├── RunningEngine.kt
│   │   └─ Central coordinator aggregating all metrics (distance, speed, pace, 
│   │      cadence, HR); applies smoothing and adaptive pace calculation
│   ├── DistanceCalculator.kt
│   │   └─ Haversine-based GPS distance accumulation (0.5m noise threshold)
│   ├── SpeedCalculator.kt
│   │   └─ Instantaneous speed from GPS deltas
│   ├── PaceCalculator.kt
│   │   └─ Pace seconds/km from speed
│   ├── PaceSmoother.kt
│   │   └─ 5-sample moving average with outlier rejection
│   ├── AdaptivePaceCalculator.kt
│   │   └─ Switches between GPS mode and cadence mode; uses stride learner
│   ├── StrideLengthLearner.kt
│   │   └─ GPS-based stride calibration with persistence (SharedPrefs)
│   └── StopDetector.kt
│       └─ Cadence-based motion detection
│
├── data/
│   ├── RunningMetrics.kt
│   │   └─ Immutable data class: elapsed, distance, pace, HR, cadence, lat/lon
│   └── db/
│       ├── WorkoutSession.kt (entity)
│       ├── WorkoutSample.kt (entity)
│       ├── WorkoutDatabase.kt (Room DB)
│       └── WorkoutDao.kt
│
├── ble/
│   ├── RLensProtocol.kt
│   │   └─ BLE packet creation (metric IDs, UUIDs, Little-Endian UINT32 encoding)
│   ├── RLensConnection.kt
│   │   └─ GATT client, characteristic discovery, write queue, reconnect logic
│   └── RLensScanner.kt
│       └─ BLE LE device discovery (filters "rlens" name), MAC binding, retry strategy
│
└── ui/
    ├── screens/
    │   ├── HomeScreen.kt
    │   │   └─ Start button, connection status display (colors per state)
    │   └── RunningScreen.kt
    │       └─ 2×2 metrics grid (pace, cadence, distance, HR), pause/stop buttons, 
    │          touch-to-brighten, ambient mode variant
    ├── components/
    │   └── MetricItem.kt
    │       └─ Icon + value composable
    └── theme/
        ├── Color.kt
        └── Theme.kt
```

---

### **2. WORKOUT/EXERCISE TYPE**

**How the app starts a workout:**

1. **User taps START on HomeScreen** → `MainActivity.startRunning()` (MainActivity.kt:308)
2. **Service is started**: `startForegroundService(ExerciseService)` (MainActivity.kt:313)
3. **Service binds ExerciseManager**: `exerciseManager.startExercise()` (ExerciseService.kt:333)

**Health Services ExerciseType:**
- **Type**: `ExerciseType.RUNNING` (hardcoded)
- **Location**: ExerciseManager.kt:198
  ```kotlin
  val config = ExerciseConfig.builder(ExerciseType.RUNNING)
      .setDataTypes(dataTypes)
      .setIsAutoPauseAndResumeEnabled(false)
      .setIsGpsEnabled(true)
      .build()
  exerciseClient.startExerciseAsync(config).await()
  ```

**Configuration flow:**
- ExerciseService.onCreate() → ExerciseManager(context) (ExerciseService.kt:114)
- ExerciseManager.startExercise() checks device capabilities and builds config (ExerciseManager.kt:147–214)
- Supported data types requested: HEART_RATE_BPM, LOCATION, STEPS_PER_MINUTE, DISTANCE_TOTAL, STEPS

---

### **3. METRICS COLLECTION**

**Metrics collected and their sources:**

| Metric | Source | Handler | Notes |
|--------|--------|---------|-------|
| **Heart Rate (bpm)** | Health Services HEART_RATE_BPM | ExerciseManager callback → ExerciseService.kt:115 → RunningEngine.updateHeartRate(hr) | Real-time, update every ~500ms |
| **Distance (meters)** | Health Services DISTANCE_TOTAL | ExerciseManager callback → ExerciseService.kt:129 → RunningEngine.updateDistance(meters) | Sensor-fused (GPS + accel + step calib), most accurate |
| **Pace (sec/km)** | Calculated from distance delta + time | RunningEngine.updateDistance() → AdaptivePaceCalculator (RunningEngine.kt:223) | Via 5-sample smoother; fallback to cadence mode |
| **Speed (m/s)** | Calculated from GPS delta | RunningEngine.updateGps() → SpeedCalculator (RunningEngine.kt:140) | Only when Health Services distance unavailable |
| **Cadence (spm)** | Health Services STEPS_PER_MINUTE | ExerciseManager callback → ExerciseService.kt:126 → RunningEngine.updateCadence(spm) | For adaptive pace and stride learning |
| **Steps (count)** | Health Services STEPS (real platform deltas) | ExerciseManager callback → ExerciseService.kt:134 → RunningEngine.updateStepsDelta(steps) | Switches off wall-clock reconstruction (RunningEngine.kt:252) |
| **Elapsed time (sec)** | 1Hz timer tick | ExerciseService startTimer() → RunningEngine.tick() (ExerciseService.kt:436) | Increments while running |
| **Location (lat/lon)** | Health Services LOCATION | ExerciseManager callback → RunningEngine.updateGps() (ExerciseService.kt:121) | GPS, used for Haversine distance calc |
| **Calories** | ❌ Not collected | — | Not requested in data types |

**Metrics formatting (RunningMetrics.kt):**
- **elapsedFormatted**: M:SS (line 12–17)
- **paceFormatted**: M:SS (line 19–25)
- **distanceKmFormatted**: "X.Y km" with explicit formatting (line 27–34)

**Accuracy notes:**
- Distance: Health Services sensor fusion (GPS + accelerometer + step calibration) takes precedence (RunningEngine.kt:138, 208–242)
- Pace: Updated via AdaptivePaceCalculator from distance deltas; 3-second validity window to prevent flickering (RunningEngine.kt:300–305)
- Cadence: Used for stride learning when GPS unavailable (RunningEngine.kt:183–196)

---

I can put together mockups, diagrams, comparisons, and other visuals as we go (e.g. how the running/cycling toggle and the cycling metrics screen would look on the round Wear OS display). message.content: 그래서 어떻게 하자는 거냐?

content.text:
You didn't pick up the visual-companion offer and you want the plan — so, text-only, here's the concrete proposal. Two decisions genuinely reshape the whole thing; everything else follows from them.

## Proposed approach

**Branch:** `feat/cycling-mode` (mirrors what runvision-iq did) → merge to `main` later.

**Running mode = frozen.** `RunningEngine`, all `engine/*Calculator` files, `RLensProtocol`, `RLensConnection`, `RunningScreen` — **0 lines changed**. Running's runtime behavior stays byte-identical.

**New parallel cycling stack (all new files):**
- `ui/screens/CyclingScreen.kt` — its own 2×2 grid: **Speed / Distance / HR / Elapsed**
- `engine/CyclingEngine.kt` — speed from Health Services / GPS; no pace-smoother, no stride-learner (those are running-only)
- Reuse `RLensProtocol` **unchanged** — same 5-byte packet format, just feed cycling values into existing slots so the rLens firmware needs no change

**Two shared touch-points that must take a small *additive* change** (this is the tension with "수정 하나도 하지마"):
- `HomeScreen.kt` — add a second button (달리기 / 자전거). Running's START path unchanged.
- `ExerciseManager.kt:198` — `ExerciseType.RUNNING` is hardcoded; needs a parameter defaulting to RUNNING.
- `WorkoutSession.exerciseType` already exists (defaults `"RUNNING"`) — no schema change, just write `"CYCLING"`.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
- **The "수정 하나도 하지마" constraint can't be literal.** A button "on the screen" means `HomeScreen` *must* gain a widget, and `ExerciseManager` hardcodes `ExerciseType.RUNNING` at line 198. runvision-iq faced the same wall and chose *additive, behavior-preserving* changes (it did modify `RunVisionIQView.mc` minimally). The real question is which interpretation you want.
- **Wear OS hardware gap vs. runvision-iq:** Garmin watches have barometers, so iq cycling maps the cadence slot → altitude. Most Wear OS watches have **no barometer**, and bike cadence needs an external sensor. So we can't faithfully mirror iq's altitude/cadence slots — we'd be sending fake zeros.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

Those two facts are exactly the two decisions I need from you:

aiTitle: KC and CB certification process for China

content.text:
확정된 두 결정으로 설계가 잠깁니다: **추가만 허용 + runvision-iq 완전 미러**. 이제 구체 설계를 제시합니다.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
- **"완전 미러"의 숨은 함의:** rLens 펌웨어는 슬롯 라벨이 고정이므로, Wear 자전거 패킷의 *바이트가 runvision-iq 자전거 패킷과 동일*해야 HUD가 같게 보입니다. 즉 단순히 "비슷한 값"이 아니라 runvision-iq `CyclingStrategy`의 스케일링(속도 km/h 인코딩, 고도 m, HR/누적상승 30초 락)을 **바이트 단위로 복제**해야 합니다 — 이게 스펙의 핵심 검증 항목이 됩니다.
- **바로미터 없는 Wear에서도 고도는 가능:** GPS LOCATION에 altitude가 실려 옵니다(정확도 낮음). 그래서 "가짜 0"이 아니라 *GPS 고도*로 0x0E 슬롯과 누적상승을 채울 수 있습니다 — 정확도 caveat는 있지만 미러는 성립합니다.
- **정직한 3번째 접점:** 앞서 HomeScreen·ExerciseManager 2곳을 말했는데, `ExerciseService`의 콜백→엔진 라우팅도 모드 분기가 필요합니다. 러닝 분기는 오늘 코드와 바이트 동일하게 두고 `if(cycling)`만 추가 — runvision-iq가 View에서 한 것과 같은 방식입니다.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

## 설계 (1차 제시)

### 1. 모드 진입
`HomeScreen`에 버튼 2개: **달리기 / 자전거**. 달리기 버튼은 현재 START 경로 그대로(0 변경), 자전거 버튼만 신규. `MainActivity` 네비게이션에 `"cycling"` 라우트 추가(`"running"` 라우트 무수정).

### 2. 동작 분기 (러닝 = 기본값, 무변경)
| 공유 파일 | 추가 내용 | 러닝 영향 |
|---|---|---|
| `HomeScreen.kt` | 자전거 버튼 1개 | 없음 (START 그대로) |
| `MainActivity.kt` | `"cycling"` 라우트 | 없음 |
| `ExerciseManager.kt:198` | `exerciseType: ExerciseType = RUNNING` 파라미터 | 기본값=RUNNING → 동일 |
| `ExerciseService.kt` | `if(mode==CYCLING) cyclingEngine else runningEngine` 디스패치 | 러닝 분기 바이트 동일 |
| `WorkoutSession` | `exerciseType="CYCLING"` 기록 | 스키마 이미 존재, 무변경 |

### 3. 신규 파일 (병렬 스택)
- `engine/CyclingEngine.kt` — Health Services `BIKING` 콜백 소비. 산출: elapsed, distance(m), speed(km/h, HS SPEED 우선·GPS 폴백), 현재 GPS altitude(m), totalAscent(양의 고도 델타 누적), HR. **30초 HR 락**: `elapsed>=30` 시점에 `0x0B`가 HR이냐 totalAscent냐 영구 고정 (iq 로직 미러). 페이스 스무더·스트라이드 러너 **없음**.
- `ui/screens/CyclingScreen.kt` — 2×2 + Ambient 변형(Wear 품질 가이드 준수, RunningScreen 미러). **워치 화면은 정직한 라벨**: Speed / Distance / HR / Ascent(또는 Altitude) + Elapsed. 슬롯 리매핑은 BLE 계층에서만.
- `ble/CyclingPacketMapper.kt` — RLensProtocol을 **무수정 재사용**. 슬롯 ID·`[id][u32 LE]` 포맷 동일, 값만 자전거 의미로 매핑: `0x03`=elapsed, `0x06`=distance, `0x07`=speed, `0x0B`=HR|totalAscent, `0x0E`=altitude. **스케일링은 runvision-iq `CyclingStrategy`와 바이트 일치하도록 복제** (스펙에서 골든 벡터로 검증).

### 4. 회귀 방지 = 가장 강한 보증
러닝 무변경의 증거는 **git diff**: `RunningEngine`, `engine/*Calculator*`, `RLensProtocol`, `RLensConnection`, `RunningScreen` = diff 0줄. + 테스트: 러닝 경로 BLE 바이트가 변경 전과 동일.

### 5. 테스트 (JUnit, iq 케이스 미러)
`CyclingEngine`: 속도 변환, 거리, totalAscent 누적, 30초 락 4종(T30 전 HR / T30 HR 유지 / T30 HR 없으면 ascent / 락 후 HR 복귀해도 ascent 유지). + **패킷 패리티**: 자전거 패킷 ↔ runvision-iq 인코딩 골든 벡터 바이트 일치.

### 6. 범위 밖 (Phase 1 제외)
타일 서비스, **versionCode 증가**(폰 앱과 공유 → 릴리즈 시점 별도 결정, 기능 브랜치에서 안 건드림).

### 미결 사항 (구현 단계 확정)
- runvision-iq `CyclingStrategy`의 정확한 속도/고도 인코딩 스케일 → 스펙 작성 시 소스 교차 확인 후 골든 벡터 확정
- 워치 화면 4번째 슬롯: Ascent vs 현재 Altitude 표시 (BLE는 미러 고정, 화면 표시만 결정)

이 설계가 맞는 방향이면 스펙 문서(`runvision-wear/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-16-cycling-mode-design.md`)로 작성하겠습니다. 수정할 부분 있나요?

message.content: 심박수가 항상 들어오므로 누적상승은 사용할 일이 없을텐데?

content.text:
맞습니다. 정확한 지적입니다 — 그 폴백은 Wear에서 **도달 불가능한 죽은 코드**입니다.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
- **runvision-iq가 totalAscent 폴백을 둔 이유(Chesterton's Fence):** Garmin **Edge 시리즈(자전거 거치형 컴퓨터)는 광혈류 HR 센서가 아예 없습니다.** iq는 워치+Edge를 같은 코드로 커버해야 하므로 "HR 없으면 누적상승으로 대체"가 필요했습니다.
- **Wear에는 그 기기 클래스가 없습니다.** 이 앱은 손목 워치 전용이고, Wear OS 워치는 광혈류 HR이 표준 장착 + 운동 세션 중 항상 활성. 따라서 `_hrEverSeen`은 30초 시점에 **항상 true** → `0x0B`는 **항상 HR**.
- **"완전 미러"와 모순 아님:** 미러의 본질은 *rLens가 받는 바이트 동일*. iq도 HR이 잡히는 정상 케이스에선 `0x0B`에 HR을 보냅니다. Wear는 그 케이스만 발생하므로 **바이트 동일 = HUD 동일**이 그대로 성립. 폴백 제거는 결정 번복이 아니라 죽은 분기 제거입니다.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

## 설계 단순화 (반영)

**제거:** 30초 HR 락 상태머신(`_hrEverSeen`/`_slotLocked`/`_useAscent`), totalAscent 누적 계산, 락 관련 테스트 4종.

**유지 (iq 바이트 미러 그대로):**

| 슬롯 | 자전거 값 |
|---|---|
| `0x03` | elapsed |
| `0x06` | distance (m) |
| `0x07` | speed (km/h) |
| `0x0B` | **HR (항상)** |
| `0x0E` | 현재 GPS altitude (m) |

`CyclingEngine`이 단순해집니다: 락 로직·누적상승 없음, HR은 러닝과 동일하게 들어오는 값 그대로(일시 드롭 시 0/직전값 — 러닝 모드와 동일 처리).

**미결 사항도 1개 해소:** 워치 화면 4번째 슬롯은 totalAscent가 사라졌으니 고민 없이 **현재 Altitude** 표시 (0x0E와 의미 일치). → 남는 미결: runvision-iq의 속도/고도 인코딩 스케일 골든 벡터 확정 1건뿐.

이렇게 단순화된 설계로 스펙 문서를 작성할까요? (`runvision-wear/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-16-cycling-mode-design.md`)

lastPrompt: 심박수가 항상 들어오므로 누적상승은 사용할 일이 없을텐데?

content.content:
1	package com.runvision.wear.ble
2	
3	import java.util.UUID
4	
5	/**
6	 * rLens BLE Protocol Implementation
7	 * Based on rLens BLE V1.0.1 Specification
8	 *
9	 * Packet Format: [Metric_ID (1 byte)] [Value (4 bytes, Little-Endian UINT32)]
10	 *
11	 * Reference: runvision-iq/source/ILensProtocol.mc (Garmin Monkey C implementation)
12	 */
13	object RLensProtocol {
14	
15	    // ========== Service UUIDs ==========
16	    val EXERCISE_SERVICE_UUID: UUID = UUID.fromString("4b329cf2-3816-498c-8453-ee8798502a08")
17	    val CONFIG_SERVICE_UUID: UUID = UUID.fromString("58211c97-482a-2808-2d3e-228405f1e749")
18	
19	    // ========== Characteristic UUIDs ==========
20	    val EXERCISE_DATA_UUID: UUID = UUID.fromString("c259c1bd-18d3-c348-b88d-5447aea1b615")
21	    val UNIVERSAL_SUBSCRIPTIONS_UUID: UUID = UUID.fromString("c1329ce5-b463-31a5-8b78-bd220c1480cd")
22	    val CURRENT_TIME_UUID: UUID = UUID.fromString("54ac7f82-eb87-aa4e-0154-a71d80471e6e")
23	    val BATTERY_LEVEL_UUID: UUID = UUID.fromString("33bd4a32-f763-0391-2820-55610f999aef")
24	
25	    // ========== Metric IDs ==========
26	    const val METRIC_ELAPSED_TIME = 0x01    // Elapsed time in seconds
27	    const val METRIC_DISTANCE = 0x06        // Distance in meters
28	    const val METRIC_VELOCITY = 0x07        // Pace in seconds/km
29	    const val METRIC_HEART_RATE = 0x0B      // Heart rate in bpm
30	    const val METRIC_CADENCE = 0x0E         // Cadence in steps per minute
31	
32	    // Additional metric IDs from spec
33	    const val METRIC_RECORD_STATUS = 0x01   // 0: Start, 1: Pause, 2: End
34	    const val METRIC_HEAT_DISSIPATION = 0x02
35	    const val METRIC_EXERCISE_TIME = 0x03
36	    const val METRIC_TOTAL_TIME = 0x04
37	    const val METRIC_PAUSE_TIME = 0x05
38	    const val METRIC_AVG_MOVEMENT_SPEED = 0x08
39	    const val METRIC_AVG_SPEED = 0x09
40	    const val METRIC_MAX_SPEED = 0x0A
41	    const val METRIC_AVG_HEART_RATE = 0x0C
42	    const val METRIC_MAX_HEART_RATE = 0x0D
43	    const val METRIC_MAX_CADENCE = 0x0F
44	    const val METRIC_AVG_CADENCE = 0x10
45	    const val METRIC_POWER = 0x11
46	    const val METRIC_MAX_POWER = 0x12
47	    const val METRIC_AVG_POWER = 0x13
48	
49	    /**
50	     * Create metric packet with Little-Endian UINT32 encoding
51	     *
52	     * @param metricId Metric identifier (see METRIC_* constants)
53	     * @param value Integer value to encode (clamped to [0, Int.MAX_VALUE])
54	     * @return ByteArray 5-byte packet [MetricID, Value_LSB, ..., Value_MSB]
55	     */
56	    fun createPacket(metricId: Int, value: Int): ByteArray {
57	        // Clamp value to safe range [0, Int.MAX_VALUE]
58	        val safeValue = value.coerceIn(0, Int.MAX_VALUE)
59	
60	        return byteArrayOf(
61	            metricId.toByte(),
62	            (safeValue and 0xFF).toByte(),           // Byte 0 (LSB)
63	            ((safeValue shr 8) and 0xFF).toByte(),   // Byte 1
64	            ((safeValue shr 16) and 0xFF).toByte(),  // Byte 2
65	            ((safeValue shr 24) and 0xFF).toByte()   // Byte 3 (MSB)
66	        )
67	    }
68	
69	    /**
70	     * Create elapsed time packet
71	     * @param seconds Elapsed time in seconds
72	     */
73	    fun createElapsedTimePacket(seconds: Int): ByteArray =
74	        createPacket(METRIC_ELAPSED_TIME, seconds)
75	
76	    /**
77	     * Create distance packet
78	     * @param meters Distance in meters
79	     */
80	    fun createDistancePacket(meters: Int): ByteArray =
81	        createPacket(METRIC_DISTANCE, meters)
82	
83	    /**
84	     * Create pace/velocity packet
85	     * @param secondsPerKm Pace in seconds per kilometer (e.g., 292 = 4:52/km)
86	     */
87	    fun createPacePacket(secondsPerKm: Int): ByteArray =
88	        createPacket(METRIC_VELOCITY, secondsPerKm)
89	
90	    /**
91	     * Create heart rate packet
92	     * @param bpm Heart rate in beats per minute
93	     */
94	    fun createHeartRatePacket(bpm: Int): ByteArray =
95	        createPacket(METRIC_HEART_RATE, bpm)
96	
97	    /**
98	     * Create cadence packet
99	     * @param spm Cadence in steps per minute
100	     */
101	    fun createCadencePacket(spm: Int): ByteArray =
102	        createPacket(METRIC_CADENCE, spm)
103	
104	    /**
105	     * Create record status packet
106	     * @param status 0: Start, 1: Pause, 2: End
107	     */
108	    fun createRecordStatusPacket(status: Int): ByteArray =
109	        createPacket(METRIC_RECORD_STATUS, status)
110	
111	    /**
112	     * Create exercise time packet
113	     * @param seconds Exercise time in seconds
114	     */
115	    fun createExerciseTimePacket(seconds: Int): ByteArray =
116	        createPacket(METRIC_EXERCISE_TIME, seconds)
117	
118	    /**
119	     * Create power packet
120	     * @param watts Power in watts
121	     */
122	    fun createPowerPacket(watts: Int): ByteArray =
123	        createPacket(METRIC_POWER, watts)
124	
125	    // ========== Current Time Packet for Elapsed Time ==========
126	    // WORKAROUND: Send elapsed time using iLens's Current Time characteristic
127	    // This allows pause/resume to work correctly (watch controls the time display)
128	    // Reference: runvision-iq/source/ILensProtocol.mc:createCurrentTimePacket
129	
130	    /**
131	     * Create Current Time packet with elapsed time
132	     *
133	     * Packet Structure (10 bytes):
134	     *   [0-1]  year (UINT16, little-endian) - current date
135	     *   [2]    month (UINT8, 1-12) - current date
136	     *   [3]    day (UINT8, 1-31) - current date
137	     *   [4]    hour (UINT8, 0-23) - ELAPSED hours
138	     *   [5]    minute (UINT8, 0-59) - ELAPSED minutes
139	     *   [6]    second (UINT8, 0-59) - ELAPSED seconds
140	     *   [7]    dayOfWeek (UINT8, 1=Mon...7=Sun) - current
141	     *   [8]    fraction256 (UINT8, 1/256 second) - always 0
142	     *   [9]    reason (UINT8, 0x02=External Reference)
143	     *
144	     * @param elapsedSeconds Exercise elapsed time in seconds
145	     * @return ByteArray 10-byte packet
146	     */
147	    fun createCurrentTimePacket(elapsedSeconds: Int): ByteArray {
148	        val calendar = java.util.Calendar.getInstance()
149	
150	        // Year (UINT16, little-endian)
151	        val year = calendar.get(java.util.Calendar.YEAR)
152	
153	        // Month (1-12)
154	        val month = calendar.get(java.util.Calendar.MONTH) + 1
155	
156	        // Day (1-31)
157	        val day = calendar.get(java.util.Calendar.DAY_OF_MONTH)
158	
159	        // Convert elapsed seconds to H:M:S
160	        val hours = (elapsedSeconds / 3600) % 24
161	        val minutes = (elapsedSeconds % 3600) / 60
162	        val seconds = elapsedSeconds % 60
163	
164	        // DayOfWeek: Calendar uses 1=Sunday...7=Saturday
165	        // iLens expects: 1=Monday...7=Sunday
166	        val calDow = calendar.get(java.util.Calendar.DAY_OF_WEEK)
167	        val iLensDow = if (calDow == java.util.Calendar.SUNDAY) 7 else calDow - 1
168	
169	        return byteArrayOf(
170	            (year and 0xFF).toByte(),           // Year low byte
171	            ((year shr 8) and 0xFF).toByte(),   // Year high byte
172	            month.toByte(),
173	            day.toByte(),
174	            hours.toByte(),
175	            minutes.toByte(),
176	            seconds.toByte(),
177	            iLensDow.toByte(),
178	            0x00,                               // Fraction256
179	            0x02                                // Reason: External Reference
180	        )
181	    }
182	
183	    // ========== Universal Subscriptions Initialization Commands ==========
184	    // CRITICAL: Must be sent after BLE connection before sending exercise data
185	    // Reference: iLens-BLE-Connection-Best-Practices.md
186	
187	    /**
188	     * Universal Subscriptions command 1: Read version/language
189	     * Write to UNIVERSAL_SUBSCRIPTIONS_UUID, wait for NOTIFY response
190	     */
191	    fun createUniversalSubCommand1(): ByteArray = byteArrayOf(0x10, 0x01)
192	
193	    /**
194	     * Universal Subscriptions command 2: Read BT address
195	     * Write to UNIVERSAL_SUBSCRIPTIONS_UUID, wait for NOTIFY response
196	     */
197	    fun createUniversalSubCommand2(): ByteArray = byteArrayOf(0x10, 0x04)
198	
199	    /**
200	     * Universal Subscriptions command 3: Read translation settings
201	     * Write to UNIVERSAL_SUBSCRIPTIONS_UUID, wait for NOTIFY response
202	     */
203	    fun createUniversalSubCommand3(): ByteArray = byteArrayOf(0x10, 0x03)
204	}
